One by one the Apostles chosen by Christ were killed, as he had predicted (see John 16:1-4). James, the brother of John, was the first member of the Quorum of the Twelve that we know of to be executed, sometime around A.D. 44 (see Acts 12:1-2). Others followed. James the Just, the brother of the Lord,

Literally, he added to seize, he seized Peter in addition to James. {The days of unleavened bread}. By this parenthesis Luke locates the time of the year when Peter was arrested, the passover. It was a fine occasion for Agrippa to increase his favour among the crowds of Jews there by extra zeal against the
